cot_prefix: Okay, let's look for the answer to the question. The question is, what upset Melanie during the National Championships? The answer options are A to D. First, I need to find the part of the text that talks about Melanie's experience in the National Championships. Let me scan through the text again. The relevant paragraph is this part: "Melanie entered her first triathlon at 14 and she won the junior section. Full of confidence, she entered the National Championships, and although she had the second fastest swim and the fastest run, she came nowhere. 'I was following this man and suddenly we came to the sea. We realised then that we had gone wrong. I ended up cycling 20 kilometres too far. I cried all the way through the running.'" So, during the National Championships, Melanie made a mistake in the course. She was following another person, and they took a wrong turn, leading her to cycle an extra 20 kilometers. This caused her to not perform well in the race, and she ended up not placing well. Now, looking at the answer options: (A) She was tricked by another competitor. - The text says she was following "this man," but there's no indication he tricked her intentionally. It's more about a mistake or wrong direction, not a deliberate trick. So probably not A. (B) She felt she had let her teammates down. - The text doesn't mention her feeling responsible for her teammates. It's about her own performance, not affecting others. So B is not correct. (C) She made a mistake during part of the race. - Yes, she made a mistake by following the wrong path and cycling too far. This fits option C.
